rollout_stability.note: v1 rollout plateaus by step 5 (range 0.046), v0 keeps drifting (range 0.110)

embedding_norms.note: High norm mean (13.9) explains why zero baseline wins MSE — zero vector is far from embeddings, but so are predictions. The L2 distance to zero is dominated by the norm bias.

cosine_similarity.note: Low absolute cossim (0.096) is expected — embeddings are high-dimensional (192-d), random vectors have ~0 cossim. Model beats copy-last by 33%.

residual_cosine.note: This is the key metric — predicting the direction of temporal change. 0.641 is strong for unconditional prediction. v0 num_preds=6 got 0.577.

key_findings: v1 rollout curve stabilizes by step 5, v0 keeps drifting through step 12
v1 step 5-12 range is 0.046 vs v0's 0.110 — 2.4x more stable rollouts
v1 surprise std increased (0.924 -> 1.156) — better discrimination between easy/hard trajectories
v1 delta cosine 0.641 vs v0's 0.577 — 11.1% improvement in temporal direction prediction
Zero baseline (MSE 1.163) still beats model — explained by embedding norm bias (mean=13.9)
open_issues: Zero baseline outperforms in MSE — embedding mean norm is 13.9, creating trivial bias. Consider centering embeddings.
v1 vs mean-context gap narrows to ~0.4% by step 12 — model advantage fades at long horizons
Need per-regime breakdown to confirm v1 improves across all scene types
